Notes: Canonical RB-E2F/DP-CyclinE loop. RB1 inhibits E2F:TFDP1 complex; TFDP1:E2F drives CCNE1 transcription; CyclinE/CDK2 phosphorylates and inactivates RB1. Well-described oscillatory bistable switch at G1/S.

Canonical RB-E2F-CyclinE feedback loop. RB1 sequesters E2F2; E2F2 transcribes CCNE1; CyclinE/CDK2 hyperphosphorylates RB1. Subcritical Hopf bifurcation and sustained oscillations demonstrated in modeling.

SOCS3 directly inhibits JAK1 catalytic activity via KIR pseudosubstrate mechanism. JAK1 phosphorylates STAT5B. STAT5B induces SOCS genes including SOCS3 (primarily STAT3-driven but STAT5 also contributes). Classic JAK-STAT negative feedback.

SOCS3 inhibits TYK2 catalytic activity. TYK2 phosphorylates STAT2 in IFN-alpha signaling. However STAT2 is not a major direct inducer of SOCS3 (SOCS3 induction is primarily STAT3/STAT1-dependent). The closing edge STAT2->SOCS3 is weakly supported.

SOCS3 inhibits TYK2 via KIR/GQM mechanism. TYK2 phosphorylates STAT3. STAT3 is the primary transcriptional activator of SOCS3 promoter. Textbook JAK-STAT-SOCS negative feedback loop with demonstrated oscillatory potential.

SOCS3 binds JAK2 and receptor simultaneously to inhibit kinase activity. JAK2 phosphorylates STAT1. STAT1 activates SOCS3 promoter via SRE binding. Confirmed negative feedback with IFN-gamma signaling.

SOCS3 can inhibit STAT1 phosphorylation. But STAT1 does not directly activate STAT5B; they are parallel STAT proteins activated by JAKs independently. KEGG may be capturing pathway co-occurrence rather than a direct regulatory edge.

SOCS3 inhibits STAT3 phosphorylation. STAT3 can regulate STAT5A epigenetically (DNA methylation) but this is not a canonical activation edge. STAT5A can contribute to SOCS expression but primarily induces SOCS2/CIS. Edge2 weakly supported.
Same issue as STAT3->STAT5A motif. SOCS3 inhibits STAT3; STAT3 does not directly activate STAT5B (parallel pathway members). KEGG likely captures pathway co-membership not direct regulation.

Classic leptin signaling feedback. SOCS3 binds phospho-Tyr985 on LEPR to block signaling. LEPR activates JAK2 which phosphorylates STAT3. STAT3 induces SOCS3 transcription. Well-characterized feedback with 2-3hr SOCS3 induction delay enabling oscillatory dynamics.

Textbook TGF-beta negative feedback. SMAD7 inhibits SMAD3 phosphorylation and blocks SMAD3-SMAD4 complex. SMAD3 forms heterotrimers with SMAD4 for transcription. SMAD3/4 complex directly binds SMAD7 promoter SBE to induce SMAD7 expression. Delay from transcription/translation enables oscillation.

ERK2/MAPK1 phosphorylates SMAD3 linker region (inhibitory not activating). SMAD3 is a TGF-beta R-SMAD not canonically downstream of BMP receptors (SMAD1/5/8 are). BMPR1B does not directly inhibit MAPK1. Likely KEGG pathway overlap annotation artifact.

STAT3 and STAT1 are co-activated by JAKs not directly regulated by each other. STAT1 is not a well-characterized transcriptional activator of SOCS5 (SOCS5 regulation poorly characterized). SOCS5 does inhibit JAK1/STAT3 signaling. Two of three edges are weak.

STAT3 does not directly activate STAT5A (parallel pathway). STAT5A/5B are confirmed transcriptional activators of SOCS6 promoter. SOCS6 primarily targets STAT5 not STAT3 for inhibition. Two of three edges are questionable.

STAT3 does not directly activate STAT5B. STAT5B is the dominant transactivator of CISH promoter via MGF boxes. CISH primarily inhibits STAT5 signaling but some evidence for STAT3 inhibition exists. Edge1 not supported.
Same structural issue. STAT3 does not directly regulate STAT5B. STAT5B activates SOCS6 transcription. SOCS6 primarily targets STAT5 not STAT3. Two edges weak.

STAT3 can activate CISH transcription. But CISH does not activate PRLR; CISH binds phospho-PRLR to BLOCK STAT5 recruitment (inhibitory not activating). PRLR activates STAT3 not inhibits it. Edge directions are inverted from biological reality.

STAT3 may weakly induce SOCS7 but this is not well-established. SOCS7 acts via ubiquitination not direct JAK kinase inhibition. JAK1 activates STAT3 (not inhibits). The inhibitory edge JAK1-|STAT3 is biologically inverted.

STAT3 can induce SOCS1 expression. SOCS1 potently inhibits JAK2 via KIR pseudosubstrate mechanism. But JAK2 activates STAT3 (phosphorylates Tyr705); it does not inhibit STAT3. The inhibitory edge is biologically inverted -- this is actually a negative feedback loop but with edges going the opposite direction.

p21/CDKN1A inhibits CDK2 (edge is inhibitory not activating). CDK2 phosphorylates FOXO1 at S249 causing nuclear exclusion and inhibition. FOXO1 transcriptionally activates CDKN1A/p21. This is a real double-negative feedback loop but edge1 direction is inhibitory (p21 inhibits CDK2) not activating as the NFL A->B format assumes.
